_comment: Eval set for aviation-sms-builder (06-06 hse-aviation flagship #36). Intake-gate + four-pillar/SSP-alignment + the de-id CLEAN fixture case (reporter/accountable-manager identity protection). The seeded deid-leak.md (auto-discovered by test_sector_deid_pairs) proves the de-id hard-fail.

evals.query: Help us improve safety at our airline.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the structured intake; refuses to draft a manual until the named operator, the operation type, and the certificating authority / State Safety Programme are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any drafting.
Refuses to produce a generic 'aviation SMS manual' — elicits the named operator/airport/AMO and the operation type (specificity).
Captures the jurisdiction / State Safety Programme (DGCA SSP / FAA / EASA) before citing any regulatory basis.

evals.query: Build the SMS manual for our DGCA-certificated scheduled passenger operator.
evals.expected_behavior: Walks the four ICAO Annex 19 pillars in order, names the Accountable Manager and Safety Manager, aligns to the DGCA SSP, and marks the exact CAR number [GAP] rather than inventing one.
expectations: Covers all four Annex 19 pillars (Safety Policy & Objectives, Safety Risk Management, Safety Assurance, Safety Promotion) — flags any pillar with no input as [GAP].
Names the Accountable Manager and the Safety Manager (Pillar 1 key personnel).
Aligns to the DGCA SSP (KB-REG-IN-DGCA) and reports the exact CAR clause as [GAP] to verify — does NOT fabricate a CAR number (the citation grader is row-blind).

evals.query: Draft the safety policy for our operator — here are the accountable manager and safety manager details.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; the accountable-manager and safety-manager names, contacts, and any reporter detail are pseudonymised to role labels; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are listed up front.
The Accountable Manager / Safety Manager names, phone, email and address become role labels; no re-identification key is embedded.
No real name, contact, address or reporter identity in the circulated output (de_identification hard-fail on any leak).
